Author: Anthropic
Title: The Claude 3 model family: Opus, Sonnet, Haiku
Definition of desired behaviour: Having the model's assertions be in line with its knowledge and not asserting things it knows to be false; expressing uncertainty when unsure.
Training method for desired behaviour: Trained the model to output fewer claims that it can identify are false

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.6 System Card
Definition of desired behaviour: Models that provide accurate information, acknowledge uncertainty, and avoid asserting claims they cannot support; avoid hallucinating
Training method for desired behaviour: RLHF and reinforcement learning from AI feedback

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card
Definition of desired behaviour: Recognize the limitations of their knowledge and not to make claims that they know to be false.
Training method for desired behaviour: Honesty training

Author: Anthropic
Title: Claude Sonnet 4.5 System Card
Definition of desired behaviour: provide accurate information and appropriately express uncertainty when it lacks knowledge... truthful, acknowledge their limitations, and avoid making claims they cannot substantiate
Training method for desired behaviour: During reinforcement learning, Claude models are trained to be honest and not to make claims that they know to be false.

Title: Competitive Programming with Large Reasoning Models
Definition of desired behaviour: Solving complex algorithmic problems demands advanced computational thinking and problem solving skills.
Training method for desired behaviour: Reinforcement learning applied to large language models to "reason" and "think through" extended chains of thought; RL fine-tuning emphasizing challenging programming problems.

Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card

Title: Claude Opus 4.5 System Card

Author: Gemma Team
Title: Gemma model card
Definition of desired behaviour: Ability to generate English-language text in response to the input, such as an answer to a question, or a summary of a document.
Training method for desired behaviour: Pre-training and instruction tuning

Author: xAI
Title: Grok 4 Model Card
Definition of desired behaviour: factual responses are framed without significant differences in sentiment, forcefulness, verbal hedging, or positive sentiment toward one side than another
Source of definition: internal policy (xAI aims to build truth-seeking models)
Training method for desired behaviour: implementing safeguards to improve our model's political objectivity... adding explicit instructions to avoid these behaviors in the system prompt

Author: Meta
Title: Llama 3.1 model card
Definition of desired behaviour: Align with human preferences for helpfulness and safety; respects the dignity and autonomy of all users, without insertion unnecessary judgment or normativity.
Source of definition: Meta internal values (openness, inclusivity and helpfulness)
Training method for desired behaviour: supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning with human feedback (RLHF)

Author: Gemma Team
Title: Gemma 2 model card
Desired behaviour: Language understanding, generation, and reasoning capabilities
Definition of desired behaviour: Strong capabilities in language understanding, generation, and reasoning measured across standard automated benchmarks.
Training method for desired behaviour: Pre-training, knowledge distillation, and instruction fine-tuning
Benchmark: Standard Academic Benchmarks (MMLU, GSM8K, ARC-c, HellaSwag, Winogrande, MATH, ARC-e, PIQA, SIQA, Boolq, TriviaQA, NQ, HumanEval, MBPP)

Author: OpenAI
Title: OpenAI o1 System Card
Definition of desired behaviour: explicitly reason through safety specifications before producing an answer... follow specific guidelines and model policies we've set, helping them act in line with our safety expectations
Training method for desired behaviour: large-scale reinforcement learning to reason using chain of thought

Author: OpenAI
Title: Addendum to o3 and o4-mini system card: Codex
Definition of desired behaviour: Generate code that closely mirrors human style and PR preferences, adheres precisely to instructions, and iteratively runs tests until passing results are achieved.
Training method for desired behaviour: Reinforcement learning on real-world coding tasks

Author: Anthropic
